free_text: Simultaneous Measurement of the Neutron Fission and
Capture Cross Section for Uranium-235 for Neutron
Energies from 8 eV to 10 keV
Oak Ridge Electron Linac (ORELA)
235U fission chamber containing U3O8, 3.20+-0.05 g of
Isotopic composition: 234U (0.006%), 235U (99.702%),
Capture resonance at 36.7 eV is probably due to an
impurity which could not be identified with certainty.
. Flight path = 39.70 m.
235U fission chamber surrounded by large
liquid scintillator tank. Fission chamber detects
only fissions while the scintillator tank is
sensitive to gamma-rays from fission and capture.
BF3 chamber used to determine neutron flux
For flux determination.
Corrected for dead time, background, self-shielding.
Approved by G. de Saussure, 22 February 1973.
Data received on magnetic tape from G.De Saussure,
27 November 1972.
Reference changed
Converted to REACTION formalism
Converted to new date formats, lower case.
Uncertainty estimated to be about 3%.
Total estimated error about 6% above 100 eV.
Corrected energy at 121.654 eV.
BIB correction
Uncertainty estimated to be about 3% below 1 keV and
8% in the region from 1 - 10 keV.
First 3 data points removed, have pointwise
data.
Total estimated error about 6% between 100 eV and 1
keV and about 8% between 1 and 10 keV.
From 5675 to 6325 eV the capture cross section could
not be measured accurately because of a resonance in
Al.
First 3 data points removed, have
pointwise data.
MONITOR corrected
